key_insight: Cross-platform proteomics of CSF and plasma from the Target ALS Global Natural History Study identified thousands of proteins and multiple differentially expressed candidates for ALS biomarker and drug-target discovery.
clinical_relevance: These findings may help improve ALS biomarker development and support future therapeutic research, but they are early-stage and not yet clinically actionable.
method: 35-plex TMTpro mass spectrometry on CSF and plasma
Compared sporadic ALS samples with controls in a cross-sectional design
Benchmarked results against Olink proximity extension assay and validated select hits with immunoassay
evidence_signal: The study profiled 2,875 CSF proteins and 1,118 plasma proteins across control and sporadic ALS cohorts, identifying known and novel differentially expressed proteins with partial orthogonal validation.
watch_item: Because this is an initial cross-sectional biomarker discovery study, findings need longitudinal replication and independent validation before use in practice.
paper_title: The Target ALS Global Natural History Study: Cross-platform proteomics to accelerate biofluid biomarker and drug target discovery in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
